method_atom: A functional renormalization-group flow of the effective action for an M-component complex order-parameter field, integrating fluctuations scale by scale to track how couplings, correlation functions and critical exponents evolve. It gives a unified treatment across arbitrary spatial dimension d and field multiplicity M, locating a symmetry-breaking transition between a disordered and an ordered state and exposing crossovers between distinct effective dynamical regimes (e.g. a first-order versus second-order time-derivative structure) and between mean-field and dimension-dependent scaling, according to whether a characteristic probing scale is large or small relative to an intrinsic scale set by the ordered state.
object_atom: Nonrelativistic bosons at zero temperature undergoing a quantum phase transition, governed by an action with a linear time derivative, whose ordered phase corresponds to a nonzero density of (quasi)particles; the dilute versus dense regimes and the d=1 mapping onto the low-temperature phase of classical two-dimensional O(2M) models are its physics-specific features.
brief_reason: The scale-by-scale renormalization flow of a multi-component field with its symmetry-breaking transition, crossovers and critical exponents is the transferable machinery, while the zero-temperature nonrelativistic bosons with linear-time-derivative action and particle-density order parameter are the domain-specific object.
